method_atom: Particle-resolved dynamical simulation of a many-body fluid of interacting constituents confined against a planar boundary that is held at a fixed external potential and imposes mirror-image interactions on the charged constituents. From the microscopic trajectories one extracts the boundary-induced layered ordering, the correlation lengths and statistics of fluctuations in the screening layer, and the free-energy profiles of an activated transfer event across the boundary, then benchmarks these against a mean-field/continuum approximation that represents the fluctuating screening cloud only by its average.
object_atom: Water molecules together with dissolved ions in an aqueous ionic solution adjacent to a model ideally-polarizable metallic electrode held at constant electrical potential (an electrochemical interface), including the transfer of charge between the electrode and a redox species in solution.
brief_reason: The near-boundary many-body simulation that yields layering, fluctuation statistics of the screening layer, and activated-transfer free-energy curves benchmarked against a mean-field average is transferable, while the water/ions at a charged metal electrode and the electrochemical charge-transfer reaction are the domain-specific object.
